benefits.title: King's can assert its graduate premium more confidently and credibly
benefits.statement: King's can make stronger, more nuanced and evidence-informed claims about the futures its programmes make possible for students, including distinctive excellence, student pathways, outcomes and value.
benefits.beneficiary: Programme teams, departments, faculties, students, applicants, recruitment teams, senior education leaders and King's Edge
benefits.successLooksLike: Programme and institutional narratives claim graduate futures value with confidence, nuance and evidence, while remaining context-sensitive, academically owned and appropriately caveated.
evidenceOfSuccess: Programme reflections, profiles or story materials explain what programmes enable for students using evidence, context and academic judgement.
Programme teams recognise themselves in the story and feel able to use it with students, applicants, colleagues or external audiences.
Student-facing, applicant-facing or institutional claims about graduate futures are evidence-linked, context-sensitive, appropriately caveated and agreed with the relevant programme teams.
Programme and institutional stories feed recruitment, open day, programme review, graduate premium evidence or related King's Edge work without reducing programme value to a single metric.

enabledBy.outputTitle: First working graduate futures evidence pack

benefits.title: King's understands graduate futures enhancement opportunities better
benefits.statement: Programmes, departments, faculties and the institution can see where existing strengths should be amplified and where curriculum, skills, opportunity, support or articulation work could most strengthen students' futures.
benefits.beneficiary: Programme teams, departments, faculties, Careers and Employability Service, senior education leaders, King's Edge and students
benefits.successLooksLike: Programme and institutional conversations use graduate futures evidence to identify strengths, questions, support needs and focused opportunities for enhancement without treating the evidence as a deficit report, ranking or judgement on programme quality.
evidenceOfSuccess: Guided conversations capture agreed questions, practical local actions or areas for further support.
Actions or focus areas are taken into normal programme, department or faculty planning routes rather than remaining as standalone project paperwork.
Faculty-level learning and institutional synthesis draw out recurring strengths, questions, student pathways and support needs across contexts.
Shared themes inform King's Edge work, faculty planning, curriculum enhancement, skills-related activity or support priorities without creating programme comparison or league-table logic.

enabledBy.outputTitle: Examples of programme insight and story
Recommendations for related King's Edge work

measures.title: Confidence and credibility of graduate premium claims
measures.questionAnswered: Can King's and its programme teams assert graduate futures value more confidently and credibly?
measures.measure: Review of programme reflections, profile materials, story examples, institutional synthesis and programme-team feedback on whether the evidence helps colleagues claim strengths and explain value with confidence, nuance, context and appropriate caveats.
measures.owner: Heads of Department, with support from Careers and Employability Service and Mark Peace
measures.cadence: During early programme partner review and annual synthesis
measures.baseline: Programme-level graduate futures stories and graduate premium claims are currently uneven and not consistently evidence-linked.
measures.target: Programme and institutional stories used in review, recruitment, open days, student-facing contexts or graduate premium evidence work are evidence-linked, context-sensitive and agreed with relevant programme teams.

measures.title: Quality of graduate futures enhancement insight
measures.questionAnswered: Does the process help King's understand where graduate futures practice, support or articulation could be strengthened?
measures.measure: Review of guided conversation outputs, agreed questions, practical local actions, faculty-level learning, institutional synthesis and evidence that themes are interpreted responsibly and routed through normal programme, department, faculty or King's Edge planning.
measures.owner: Heads of Department, faculty education leads, Careers and Employability Service and Mark Peace
measures.cadence: Termly during supported conversations and annual synthesis
measures.baseline: Enhancement opportunities from programme-level graduate futures evidence are not yet consistently captured, synthesised or routed through local and institutional planning.
measures.target: Engaged programme groups and institutional synthesis identify strengths, questions, support needs and focused enhancement opportunities without reducing programmes to categories, rankings or deficit labels.
